docs.title: CFD Portfolio – Ajay
docs.text:
Welcome to my CFD project portfolio focused on:

  • OpenFOAM simulations
  • Thermal and fluid engineering
  • Python-based post-processing
  • Engineering validation studies

docs.title: 🟢 Thermal Fundamentals
docs.text:
• A1 – Heat Conduction
  • A2 – Thermal Diffusion
  • A3 – Forced Convection

  • Python automation
  • Engineering validation

docs.title: Governing Equation
docs.text:
The equation solved is:

∂C/∂t + ∇·(uC) = D∇²C − kC

Where:

  • C = scalar concentration
  • u = velocity field
  • D = diffusion coefficient
  • k = reaction rate

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
The solver is implemented using Basilisk C.

Key steps:

  • Initialize scalar field (Gaussian blob)
  • Define velocity field
  • Solve advection using Basilisk's advection module
  • Solve diffusion using diffusion operator
  • Apply reaction term

docs.title: Project 1: Reactive Scalar Transport
docs.text: This project simulates the transport of a scalar (concentration) in a flow field with diffusion and reaction.

docs.title: Physics
docs.text:
• Advection: transport due to flow
  • Diffusion: spreading due to gradients
  • Reaction: decay of scalar

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: To understand how numerical schemes affect stability and accuracy in CFD simulations.

docs.title: Observations
docs.text:
• The blob moves in the flow direction
  • Diffusion spreads the scalar
  • Reaction reduces the magnitude over time

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Predict drag coefficient and analyze wake structure.

docs.title: Learnings
docs.text:
• Wake sensitive to mesh resolution
  • Importance of y+ control
